payload.text:
Create a modern, enterprise-grade personal portfolio website using Angular 17+ (standalone components) and Tailwind CSS for a Senior Data Engineer with 9+ years of experience.

The website must be production-ready, scalable, modular, and follow Angular best practices used in enterprise applications.

Use a clean, minimalist, and professional design suitable for enterprise recruiters, global tech companies, and senior-level roles.

Technical Stack Requirements

Use:

Angular 17+
TypeScript
Tailwind CSS
Angular Animations
Standalone Components Architecture
SCSS or Tailwind styling
Responsive design (mobile, tablet, desktop)

Optional enhancements:

Angular Signals
Lazy loading
SEO optimized
Optimized performance

Application Structure

Create modular standalone components:

/src/app

components:

navbar
hero
about
skills
experience
projects
certifications
contact
footer

pages:

home

models:

experience.model.ts
project.model.ts
skill.model.ts

assets:

profile image
resume pdf

Sections Required
1. Navbar

Sticky top navbar with:

Logo / Name
Home
About
Skills
Experience
Projects
Contact
Resume download button

Smooth scrolling enabled.

2. Hero Section

Full-screen hero section with:

Name: Raju Bandaram
Title: Senior Data Engineer
Subtitle: 9+ Years Experience | Cloud | Data Engineering | Database Architecture

Buttons:

Download Resume
Contact Me
View Projects

Include modern animations.

3. About Section

Professional summary describing:

Senior Data Engineer with 9+ years experience in enterprise database systems, data engineering, cloud platforms, and scalable data infrastructure.

Highlight expertise in:

Database architecture
Cloud platforms
Data pipelines
Enterprise applications

4. Skills Section

Display skills grouped by category using modern cards:

Databases:

SQL Server
PostgreSQL
Oracle
MySQL
MongoDB
BigQuery

Cloud:

AWS
Google Cloud Platform
BigQuery
Databricks

Data Engineering:

ETL Pipelines
Data Warehousing
Data Modeling
Data Migration

Programming:

Python
SQL
JavaScript
HTML
CSS

Tools:

SSIS
SSRS
Databricks
Spark

Include skill animations or progress bars.

5. Experience Section

Timeline layout showing:

Lead Data Engineer – Tata Consultancy Services – 2022–Present

Senior Database Engineer – 2021–2022

Database Developer – 2019–2021

Software Engineer – 2016–2019

Each role should include responsibilities and achievements.

6. Projects Section

Create professional enterprise-level project cards:

Enterprise Data Warehouse
Cloud Data Platform using GCP BigQuery
Databricks Analytics Platform
Database Migration System

Each project must include:

Description
Technologies used
Architecture
Impact

7. Certifications Section

Display certifications:

AWS Certified Developer Associate
Generative AI Certification
PostgreSQL Certification

8. Contact Section

Include contact form with:

Name
Email
Message

Also show:

Email
Location: Hyderabad, India

9. Footer

Include:

Copyright
LinkedIn
GitHub
Email

Design Requirements

Use modern enterprise design:

Minimalist
Professional
Clean layout
Card-based UI
Subtle animations
Smooth scrolling
Professional typography

Color scheme:

Dark theme preferred OR clean white theme with blue accent.

Functional Requirements

Resume download functionality
Responsive layout
Smooth scroll navigation
Reusable Angular components
Modular architecture
Production-ready code

Code Quality Requirements

Use:

Standalone components
Strong typing
Reusable components
Proper folder structure
Clean architecture
Best Angular practices

Deliverables Required

Generate complete Angular project including:

All components
HTML templates
CSS / Tailwind styling
TypeScript logic
Models
Routing
Assets folder

Ready to run using:

npm install
ng serve

Optional Advanced Enhancements

Add:

Dark/light theme toggle
Animations using Angular Animations
Lazy loading modules
SEO optimization

**Defining the Application Scope**

I'm now zeroing in on defining the application scope. My current focus is to carefully analyze the request, which is to construct a modern, enterprise-grade personal portfolio website for Raju Bandaram. Key materials include the provided PDF/OCR content – Raju Bandaram's resume. This analysis will guide subsequent phases.


**Specifying the Technical Details**

I'm now nailing down the technical specifications. The plan is Angular 17+ (Standalone) with Tailwind CSS and TypeScript. The environment will be an "Applet" setup: JIT, Zoneless, specific file structure. I'm focusing on features such as Navbar, Hero, About, Skills, Experience (timeline), Projects, Certifications, Contact, and Footer, adhering to a professional, minimalist design. The constraints are single XML output, a strict file structure (`index.tsx`, etc.), no complicated HTML, and strictly valid HTML/Angular.

\n\n

**Parsing the Resume Details**

I've extracted the core data from Raju Bandaram's resume. This includes his name, title (Senior Data Engineer/Database Administrator), location, contact details, and experience - 8.5+ years, in a blend to address both prompt and resume. I am focusing on his work history: TATA Consultancy Services (Lead), and N. A. Sportz Interactive (Senior Associate).


**Mapping the Experience Details**

I'm now detailing Raju's complete work history, including Sheliza Technologies (Database Developer, Sep 2019 - Aug 2021), Bridge IT Solutions (Software Engineer, Oct 2016 - Aug 2019). The focus is now on mapping this history, along with skills such as SQL Server, MySQL, Oracle, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, AWS (S3, EC2), Python, and ETL, and certifications like AWS Certified Associate - Developer, AWS Partner: Gen AI Essentials, and PostgreSQL Certified.

I am also recording the core application structure (`metadata.json`, `index.tsx`, `index.html`, `src/app.component.ts`, `src/app.component.html`, etc.) This includes the use of Tailwind CDN and considering an Applet setup for the root of the application, with component details being grouped under `src/components/`.


**Developing the Website Structure**

Now I'm designing the site's layout. Components I am focusing on include a sticky header with section links, a hero section displaying name and title, and an about section summarizing my experience. Skills are categorized, experience displays as a timeline. Projects and certifications are displayed, too. Contact forms and a footer will complete the structure. I'm building with Dark theme, a slate/gray palette with blue accents and the design system will leverage a container-centered, responsive grid.


**Building the Code Iteratively**

I'm now implementing the code, starting with `metadata.json`. Next comes `index.tsx`, using Applet bootstrap and `provideZonelessChangeDetection`. Then, I'm integrating the Tailwind CDN and FontAwesome into `index.html`. My focus is `app.component.ts`, which orchestrates components. I'm choosing to define data structures and interfaces inside. Standalone components for each section, such as the hero and skills, will be created. I'm refining the "Experience" data and mapping OCR-extracted dates and descriptions into a structured array.


**Analyzing Constraint Violations**

I'm now carefully checking for potential violations. I'm focusing on those specific anti-patterns. This includes avoiding regex within HTML bindings, ensuring all tags are closed correctly, and properly integrating images using picsum.photos and icons via the FontAwesome CDN.

I'm now outlining a detailed implementation plan, mentally drafting component structures. This includes `app.component.ts`, with imports and active section tracking, and navbar and hero components with defined inputs and templates. This also extends to the design of the experience section.


**Constructing the Skills Component**

I'm now implementing the `Skills` and `Projects` components. I'm focusing on the `skills.component.ts` data structure, including categories, and the array structure for `projects.component.ts`. I'll be iterating over experience with a timeline format using `@for`. I plan to integrate these parts of the application now.

**Refactoring the Application Structure**

I've realized that the initial plan for numerous small files isn't optimal, potentially exceeding output limits. To address this, I've decided to consolidate logic within `src/app.component.ts` as the main container and a few key components in `src/components/`, while using Tailwind utility classes directly within HTML. This approach simplifies the structure and improves output.

entries.description: Main HTML file with Tailwind and Fonts

diffs.replacement:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en" class="scroll-smooth">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>Raju Bandaram - Senior Data Engineer</title>
  <script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>
  <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400;500;600;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.4.0/css/all.min.css">
  <style>
    body {
      font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif;
    }
    /* Custom Scrollbar */
    ::-webkit-scrollbar {
      width: 8px;
    }
    ::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
      background: #0f172a;
    }
    ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
      background: #334155;
      border-radius: 4px;
    }
    ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover {
      background: #475569;
    }
  </style>
</head>
<body class="bg-slate-950 text-slate-200 antialiased selection:bg-blue-500 selection:text-white">
  <app-root></app-root>
</body>
</html>

entries.description: Main application component orchestrating the portfolio
Hero section with main call to action

diffs.replacement:
import { Component, ChangeDetectionStrategy } from '@angular/core'; import { CommonModule } from '@angular/common'; @Component({ selector: 'app-hero', standalone: true, imports: [CommonModule], template: `
Available for Senior Roles

Hi, I'm Raju Bandaram

Senior Data Engineer

Specializing in Cloud Architecture, Database Engineering, and Scalable Data Pipelines with 9+ years of enterprise experience.

View Projects Contact Me
9+ Years Exp
20+ Projects
3+ Clouds
`, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush }) export class HeroComponent {}

import { Component, ChangeDetectionStrategy } from '@angular/core'; import { CommonModule } from '@angular/common'; @Component({ selector: 'app-about', standalone: true, imports: [CommonModule], template: `

About Me

I am a Senior Data Engineer with over 9 years of hands-on experience in designing and programming complex database architectures. My expertise spans across Microsoft SQL Server, PostgreSQL, and Oracle, with a strong focus on cloud integration.

Currently working as a Lead IT Analyst at TATA Consultancy Services, I specialize in analyzing client data to enhance business standards, building robust data pipelines, and architecting scalable solutions on AWS.

I possess strong analytical and troubleshooting skills, capable of resolving complex database performance issues and leading teams to deliver enterprise-grade applications.

DB Architecture

Design & development of schemas, stored procedures, and secure data structures.

Cloud Platforms

Expertise in AWS (S3, EC2) and data migration strategies.

Data Pipelines

Building efficient ETL processes and automated reporting systems.

`, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush }) export class AboutComponent {}

import { Component, ChangeDetectionStrategy } from '@angular/core'; import { CommonModule } from '@angular/common'; @Component({ selector: 'app-projects', standalone: true, imports: [CommonModule], template: `

Featured Projects

Delivering scalable enterprise solutions

@for (project of projects; track project.title) {
Enterprise

{{ project.title }}

{{ project.description }}

@for (tech of project.tech; track tech) { {{ tech }} }
Completed
}
`, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush }) export class ProjectsComponent { projects = [ { title: 'Enterprise Data Warehouse', description: 'Architected a centralized data repository integrating multiple business streams using SQL Server and SSIS. Streamlined data entry and reduced processing time by 40%.', icon: 'fas fa-server', tech: ['SQL Server', 'SSIS', 'Data Modeling', 'T-SQL'] }, { title: 'Cloud Analytics Platform', description: 'Built a scalable analytics backend on AWS EC2 & S3. Implemented automated ingestion pipelines for processing large datasets for client graphical analysis.', icon: 'fas fa-chart-line', tech: ['AWS S3', 'AWS EC2', 'Python', 'PostgreSQL'] }, { title: 'Sports Management DB', description: 'Designed and implemented high-performance database schemas for sports client applications. Handled real-time data ingestion during live events.', icon: 'fas fa-running', tech: ['PostgreSQL', 'Performance Tuning', 'Automation'] }, { title: 'Qlik Dashboard Integration', description: 'Developed complex data aggregation layers and SQL views to power executive Qlik dashboards, enabling real-time business decision making.', icon: 'fas fa-tachometer-alt', tech: ['SQL', 'Qlik', 'Data Aggregation', 'Oracle'] } ]; }

import { Component, ChangeDetectionStrategy } from '@angular/core'; import { CommonModule } from '@angular/common'; @Component({ selector: 'app-certifications', standalone: true, imports: [CommonModule], template: `

Certifications

AWS Certified Associate

Developer

AWS Partner

Generative AI Essentials

PostgreSQL Certified

Udemy

`, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush }) export class CertificationsComponent {}

import { Component, ChangeDetectionStrategy, signal } from '@angular/core'; import { CommonModule } from '@angular/common'; import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms'; @Component({ selector: 'app-contact', standalone: true, imports: [CommonModule, FormsModule], template: `

Let's Connect

I'm always interested in hearing about new projects and opportunities in Data Engineering and Architecture.

Email

Bandaram.raju@gmail.com

Location

Hyderabad, Telangana, India

Phone

+91-99518-33517

`, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush }) export class ContactComponent { formData = { name: '', email: '', message: '' }; isSubmitting = signal(false); onSubmit() { this.isSubmitting.set(true); // Simulate API call setTimeout(() => { alert(`Thanks ${this.formData.name}! Message sent (Simulation).`); this.isSubmitting.set(false); this.formData = { name: '', email: '', message: '' }; }, 1500); } }

import { Component, ChangeDetectionStrategy } from '@angular/core'; import { CommonModule } from '@angular/common'; @Component({ selector: 'app-footer', standalone: true, imports: [CommonModule], template: `

Raju Bandaram

Senior Data Engineer • Cloud Architect

© {{ currentYear }} Raju Bandaram. All rights reserved.
`, changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush }) export class FooterComponent { currentYear = new Date().getFullYear(); }
